title: Molinia maritima
intro: Molinia maritima, also known as sea sweet-grass or sea molinia, is a perennial plant native to coastal areas of Europe, characterized by its feathery leaves and purple flowers. The health benefits of Molinia maritima include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, boost immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Molinia maritima is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory complaints.
taxonomy: Molinia maritima is classified as a species of grass, belonging to the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Molinia, which consists of a single species native to the coastal regions of Europe. Within the family Poaceae, it is further classified as a member of the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. The species is also known as sea purple moor-grass, due to its unique purple tint in the summer months. As a member of the Poaceae family, Molinia maritima shares characteristics with other grasses, such as long, narrow leaves and a seed head that produces small, wind-dispersed seeds.
